{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Falkbeer Countergambit| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd| | |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | | | |pd|pl| | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= }} = Falkbeer Countergambit = ===3...e4=== After giving the pawn on d5, the e4 square is now free so Black pushes the e pawn instead of taking on f4. The idea is to ruin White's plan of opening the f file. The e4 pawn will also be quite a nuisance for White as it prevents natural development of the knight on f3. White will thus try to get rid of this pawn as soon as possible. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. e4 e5 2.f4 d5 3.exd5 e4'''
4 5 6
[[/4. d3|d3]]
Nf6		 dxe4
Nxe4		 Nf3
Bc5		 +=
[[/4. Nc3|Nc3]]
